description_long: This apartment building is a tall, cylindrical structure composed of stacked, rounded floors, each featuring prominent, semi-circular balconies that wrap around the exterior. The facade alternates between dark and light horizontal bands, giving the building a striking, modern appearance. The balconies are bordered by sleek, white railings, and the building's overall symmetry is accentuated by the uniform arrangement of these balconies on every floor. The roof area appears to have a communal or mechanical section, partially enclosed by railings. Windows are set back within the dark bands, providing privacy and shade for each unit. The building's design is reminiscent of iconic mid-century modernist architecture, emphasizing geometric repetition and functional outdoor spaces.
description: A tall, cylindrical apartment building with stacked, rounded balconies and alternating dark and light horizontal bands, featuring modern white railings and a symmetrical facade.
description_view_0: This view highlights the building's rounded balconies and the partial rooftop area, with the alternating bands and railings clearly visible.
description_view_1: The front-facing view emphasizes the symmetry of the balconies and the central alignment of the building's features.
description_view_2: This angle shows the rooftop section more prominently, including what appears to be a communal or mechanical area.
description_view_3: The rear view maintains the building's cylindrical symmetry and showcases the repeating balcony pattern.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, horizontal pattern, set back within the dark bands and aligned with each floor's balcony, creating a consistent and symmetrical appearance.
